description_long: This guard stone is a sculpted, anthropomorphic figure made from a single block of pale stone, likely marble or limestone, with a stylized head and torso. The head features a pronounced brow, a wide open mouth, and subtle ear-like protrusions, giving it a somewhat animalistic or abstract human appearance. The arms are truncated or folded close to the body, and the legs are short and slightly apart, with a groove at the base suggesting feet or a stable base. The surface is mostly smooth but shows patches of discoloration and weathering, with some areas appearing more worn or eroded, especially on the sides and back. The back of the figure has a carved line or belt-like feature across the lower torso, adding to its decorative or symbolic function. This object would serve as a robust, visually distinctive barrier in an urban setting, both functional and ornamental.
description: A pale, stylized stone figure with a pronounced head, open mouth, and short legs, showing signs of weathering and decorative carving. Its robust form and smooth, worn surface make it a striking and functional urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view shows the stylized face with a wide open mouth, prominent brow, and truncated arms, highlighting the anthropomorphic features.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals the profile of the head, the subtle ear-like protrusion, and the curvature of the back and legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the head, a carved belt-like line across the lower torso, and the stable, slightly separated legs.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the profile of the head and body, with visible surface discoloration and wear.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, near the entrances of driveways, or at the edges of historical sites to protect buildings and pedestrian areas from vehicle encroachment.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on a flat surface, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface discoloration and smoothing, especially on the sides and back, likely caused by weather exposure, vehicle contact, and occasional foot traffic. Some minor chipping and erosion are present on protruding features such as the head and base.
